key_insight: For wound-rotor synchronous motor performance prediction, neural-network-based surrogates—especially FT-Transformer—deliver the best accuracy and enable near-real-time inference.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that machine-learning surrogates can replace many expensive finite-element evaluations, accelerating WRSM design-space exploration and multi-objective optimization without relying on rare-earth elements.
method: Compared 8 ML/DL models across 4 algorithmic families
Trained on 3,351 Motor-CAD samples generated by Latin Hypercube Sampling
Used 10 random seeds and Optuna tuning; evaluated torque and efficiency prediction
evidence_signal: Across multiple metrics (R², MAE, RMSE, MAPE), FT-Transformer achieved the top single-model score with R² = 0.9928 and a prediction time of 0.33 ms.
watch_item: Surrogate accuracy may depend on the sampled design space and Motor-CAD-generated data, so generalization outside the training distribution should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Wound-Rotor Synchronous Motor Performance Prediction
